title: carduus collinus subsp. lobulatus
intro: Carduus collinus subsp. lobulatus, also known as the mountain thistle, is a subspecies of thistle plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to be effective in treating wounds and reducing pain. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infection. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy in various forms, including tea, tincture, and infusion, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
